title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

what: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegNspn500
title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegNspn500
text: ggseg 2.0 atlas package providing brain atlas data ggseg ecosystem. data-package custom functions.

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegNspn500
text: data/*.rda — Unified ggseg_atlas objects (2D + 3D one object) R/data.R — Roxygen2 documentation atlas R/ggsegNspn500-package.R — Package-level docs, imports ggseg.formats R/sysdata.rda — Internal palette data (brain_pals) data-raw/ — Scripts generated/converted atlas data

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegNspn500
text: package depends ggseg.formats provides ggseg_atlas class. atlas objects work ggseg::geom_brain() (2D) ggseg3d::ggseg3d() (3D).

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegNspn500
text: Update docs: devtools::document() Run tests: devtools::test() (testthat edition 3, describe/style) Full check: devtools::check() Build pkgdown site: pkgdown::build_site()

title: Quick instructions for AI coding agents working on ggsegNspn500
text: code comments except necessary workarounds Roxygen2 markdown enabled Tests use describe/BDD style vdiffr visual regression NEWS.md tracks user-facing changes (CI/pkgdown changes) URLs point ggseg GitHub org (LCBC-UiO)

what: NSPN500 Cortical Atlas for the ggseg Ecosystem
title: NSPN500 Cortical Atlas for the ggseg Ecosystem
text: package contains dataset plotting nspn500 atlas ggseg ggseg3d. Whitaker, K. J., Vértes, P. E., Romero-Garcia, R., Váša, F., Moutoussis, M., Prabhu, G., … & Tait, R. (2016). Adolescence associated genomically patterned consolidation hubs human brain connectome. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 113(32), 9105-9110. Romero-Garcia, R., Atienza, M., Clemmensen, L. H., & Cantero, J. L. (2012). Effects network resolution topological properties human neocortex. Neuroimage, 59(4), 3522-3532. learn use atlases, please look documentation ggseg ggseg3d

title: NSPN500 Cortical Atlas for the ggseg Ecosystem
text: recommend installing ggseg-atlases ggseg r-universe: can install released version ggsegNspn500 GitHub :   Please note ‘ggsegNspn500’ project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.
code: # Enable this universe options(repos = c(     ggsegverse = 'https://ggsegverse.r-universe.dev',     CRAN = 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))  # Install some packages install.packages('ggsegNspn500') # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ggsegverse/ggsegNspn500") library(ggseg) #> Warning: package 'ggseg' was built under R version 4.1.1 #> Loading required package: ggplot2 library(ggseg3d) library(ggsegNspn500)  plot(nspn500) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom",          legend.text = element_text(size = 9)) +   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 5)) library(dplyr) ggseg3d(atlas = nspn500_3d) %>%    add_glassbrain() %>%    pan_camera("right lateral")

title: ggsegNspn500 2.0.0
text: nspn500 now ggseg_atlas object (ggseg.formats) containing 2D 3D data. separate nspn500_3d object removed. Use ggplot() + ggseg::geom_brain(atlas = nspn500) 2D plots ggseg3d::ggseg3d(atlas = nspn500) 3D plots — object. ggseg.formats now hard dependency (Depends). Package URLs updated LCBC-UiO ggseg GitHub organisation.
